pipelineContext.topic: Role expiration documentation for Xperience by Kentico

signal.description: Only 'create' task in the run required 2 attempts. Style rejection for INV-style-125 (contraction inconsistency), INV-style-126 (contraction mixing), INV-style-130 (wrong UI verb 'check' vs 'select'). Accuracy rejection for wrong using directives (CODE-IMPORT-001: CMS.Core doesn't resolve IDateTimeNowService; CODE-IMPORT-002: CMS doesn't resolve RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute). Additionally, IDateTimeNowService was in CMS.Core.Internal namespace, conflicting with INV-general-012 — docFact FIND-051 was discarded and DateTime.UtcNow used instead. High invariant count (100) and acceptance criteria (23) correlated with multi-cycle outcome.

rootCauses.description: Contraction mixing in same sentence — inconsistent use of contractions
rootCauses.fix: Made contractions consistent throughout affected sentences

rootCauses.description: Used 'check' instead of 'select' for UI interaction description
rootCauses.fix: Replaced with input-agnostic verb per style guide

rootCauses.description: Wrong using directive: 'using CMS;' doesn't resolve RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute
IDateTimeNowService lives in CMS.Core.Internal namespace — conflicts with INV-general-012 (.Internal APIs must not be documented)
rootCauses.fix: Removed all IDateTimeNowService references; used DateTime.UtcNow with testability tip recommending .NET TimeProvider

signal.description: First-attempt acceptance with approved-with-notes verdict from style reviewer. Notes flagged minor contraction inconsistency ('is not' vs 'doesn't' read as intentional emphasis) and pre-existing gerund heading not introduced by this task. PAT-001 (focused single-persona developer update) confirmed effective.

styleNotes: Minor contraction inconsistency: 'is not' (line 127) vs 'doesn't' (lines 65, 129) — reads as intentional emphasis, not a pattern of formality
Pre-existing heading 'Changing role assignments for signed-in members' uses gerund form, not imperative — not a T-002 change, noted for future cleanup

gapRemediation.description: Converted 7 preexisting en-dash (U+2013) characters to -- per INV-style-096

signal.description: Required 3 attempts: (1) duplicate card insertion — two info blocks linking to same page, rejected by both style and accuracy reviewers; (2) duplicate removed; (3) gap remediation for 7 pre-existing en-dashes. The duplicate card issue is a structural error where the writer inserted the new content at two locations instead of one.

rootCauses.description: Inserted duplicate card — two info blocks both linking to member_role_expiration_xp. Second card also had inconsistent collection parameter.
rootCauses.fix: Removed the second duplicate card block (lines 99-105), kept only the first at lines 83-89

signal.description: Narrow cross-reference update (5 acceptance criteria) with PAT-009 achieved first-attempt acceptance. Tip callout added near scheduled tasks section pointing to role expiration as real-world example. Matches PAT-009 pattern for cross-ref update tasks.

Rejected solely for INV-style-096 — 3 pre-existing en-dashes in the page that the writer's initial pass did not convert. The new callout content itself was clean. This matches the en-dash detection anti-pattern: writers must scan the ENTIRE page for pre-existing en-dashes when modifying any page, not just the new content.

rootCauses.description: 3 pre-existing en-dashes (U+2013) at lines 17, 35, 260 were not converted. New callout was clean but the full-page scan requirement of INV-style-096 was not met.

gapRemediation.description: Verified en-dash conversion already performed by T-018. Full-file scan confirmed zero en-dashes remain.

signal.description: First attempt was reviewer-approved. Second attempt was gap remediation verification — confirmed T-018 had already converted en-dashes in this file. No actual content changes needed in attempt 2. Effectively a first-attempt success with gap verification overhead.

First attempt was reviewer-approved. Second attempt was a voluntary enhancement adding explicit statement about shared page/content-item access check mechanism (FIND-030). Not a rejection-driven multi-cycle — coordinator requested tighter alignment with acceptance criterion #2. PAT-012 (mirror existing page structure) and PAT-001 cited.

Business-persona cross-reference update achieved first-attempt acceptance. Applied INV-persona-013/014 for business user tone. Used business-friendly language ('premium subscription for 30 days') rather than technical jargon. PAT-009 cited. Demonstrates that persona-appropriate language + narrow scope enables first-attempt success even for cross-persona tasks.

Business-persona reference update with comprehensive persona invariant application (5 persona invariants). First-attempt acceptance. Applied the most persona invariants of any task in the run, correlating with clean persona review.

Minimal-scope reference page update (4 acceptance criteria) with first-attempt acceptance. Applied INV-general-012 (.Internal API boundary) proactively, avoiding the namespace issue that caught T-001.
Business-persona update with first-attempt acceptance. Subscription example language matches T-008 approach. Minimal scope (4 criteria) with focused persona invariant application.
First-attempt acceptance with approved-with-notes. Style reviewer verified sign-in/sign-out terminology correctness (INV-style-059, INV-style-148). Zero issues detected in new content.

Minimum-scope reference update (3 acceptance criteria) with first-attempt acceptance. Style reviewer noted pre-existing INV-style-140 violation in note callout on line 49 — not attributable to T-013 content-writer. Approved-with-notes.

styleNotes: Pre-existing INV-style-140 violation in note callout on line 49 — not attributable to T-013 content-writer, noted for future cleanup

signal.description: Narrow update (3 criteria) with PAT-009, first-attempt acceptance. Clarified that binding-style classes can have additional columns beyond FK pairs.

Multi-persona (developer+admin) reference update with highest invariant count (30) among cross-ref tasks. First-attempt acceptance. Comprehensive cross-reference invariant coverage (5 INV-crossref-* rules including INV-crossref-004 placeholder check).
Minimal cross-reference update (3 criteria, 8 invariants) with first-attempt acceptance. Contrasted standalone CRUD example with binding-style + scheduled processing.
First-attempt acceptance using both PAT-009 (cross-ref update) and PAT-012 (mirror existing page structure). Contrasted event-driven vs scheduled task approach.

gapRemediation.description: Converted en-dashes in heading 'Attribute Tag Helpers -- Images' in reference-tag-helpers.md and updated corresponding anchor= attributes in retrieve-content-items.md and content-types.md

signal.description: Gap remediation task for en-dash conversion in heading + anchor updates. First-attempt acceptance. Properly identified 2 deferred tasks in _guides collection that cannot be modified per INV-structure-030. Demonstrates correct invariant-aware scoping.

candidatePatterns.name: narrow-scope-crossref-update-with-PAT-009
candidatePatterns.description: Cross-reference update tasks with narrow scope (3-9 acceptance criteria) consistently achieved first-attempt acceptance. PAT-009 was cited by 8 of 10 first-attempt update tasks. The pattern combines minimal content insertion (single callout, tip box, or paragraph) with rigorous cross-reference invariant application (INV-crossref-002/003/005/009). All 10 tasks passed without reviewer rejection.

candidatePatterns.name: comprehensive-persona-invariant-application
candidatePatterns.description: Business-persona tasks that applied INV-persona-013/014 (business user tone) with persona-appropriate language examples (e.g., 'premium subscription for 30 days' instead of technical terms) achieved 100% first-attempt acceptance. T-009 applied the most persona invariants (5) and achieved the cleanest result. Proactive persona invariant application prevents persona reviewer rejections.

candidatePatterns.name: mirror-existing-structure-with-PAT-012
candidatePatterns.description: Tasks citing PAT-012 (mirror existing page structure when inserting callouts) both achieved reviewer approval on first attempt. When new content follows the existing page's structural patterns, style reviewers have fewer friction points.

candidatePatterns.name: docfact-vs-invariant-conflict-resolution
candidatePatterns.description: T-001 successfully resolved a docFact-vs-invariant conflict (FIND-051 mandated IDateTimeNowService but INV-general-012 prohibits .Internal APIs). The writer correctly applied invariant supremacy, discarded the docFact, and substituted DateTime.UtcNow with a testability tip. This resolution was accepted on attempt 2. Pattern: when docFacts conflict with invariants, always apply invariant supremacy and document the discarded fact with reasoning.

candidateAntiPatterns.name: en-dash-detection-failure-in-existing-content
candidateAntiPatterns.description: INV-style-096 requires converting ALL en-dashes on a page, not just in new content. T-005 was rejected because 3 pre-existing en-dashes at lines 17, 35, 260 were not converted. T-003 required gap remediation (attempt 3) to convert 7 pre-existing en-dashes. T-006 required verification that T-018 had already converted en-dashes. The writer consistently failed to perform full-page Unicode scanning before submission. This is the most impactful anti-pattern in this run — it caused 3 of the 5 multi-cycle outcomes.
candidateAntiPatterns.fix: Writers must perform a full-page binary scan (grep -P '\x{2013}') for en-dash characters before marking any update task as complete, regardless of whether the new content contains dashes.

candidateAntiPatterns.name: duplicate-content-block-insertion
candidateAntiPatterns.description: T-003 inserted TWO card blocks linking to the same target page — duplicate content at two locations in the file. Both style and accuracy reviewers independently flagged the issue. The second card also had an inconsistent collection parameter. Root cause: writer inserted content at what appeared to be two valid insertion points without checking that both reference the same target.
candidateAntiPatterns.fix: Before inserting content blocks, scan the entire target section for existing references to the same identifier. Only insert at one location.

candidateAntiPatterns.name: code-sample-import-resolution-errors
candidateAntiPatterns.description: T-001 had two code import errors: (1) 'using CMS;' doesn't resolve RegisterScheduledTaskAttribute (correct: 'using CMS.Scheduler;'), (2) 'using CMS.Core;' doesn't resolve IDateTimeNowService (it's in CMS.Core.Internal). These are compilation-breaking errors that the accuracy reviewer caught. The 'create' task with code samples is particularly vulnerable to namespace resolution errors because the writer must construct complete, compilable code examples.
candidateAntiPatterns.fix: For code samples, verify each using directive resolves the referenced types. Cross-check against docFacts for namespace information. When a type's namespace is .Internal, do not document it at all per INV-general-012.

styleEvolutions.observation: Pre-existing gerund headings detected in T-002 style review — 'Changing role assignments for signed-in members' uses gerund form instead of imperative (INV-style-103 requires imperative)
styleEvolutions.description: Style reviewer noted a pre-existing heading that uses gerund form ('Changing...') instead of imperative ('Change...'). Not introduced by this pipeline run. Flagged for future cleanup. Only 1 occurrence in reviewed content — insufficient to establish a pattern.

styleEvolutions.observation: Pre-existing INV-style-140 violation detected in T-013 style review — callout title that duplicates the callout type name
styleEvolutions.description: Style reviewer flagged pre-existing INV-style-140 violation in note callout on line 49 of IInfoProvider reference page. Not attributable to T-013 content-writer. Signals pre-existing technical debt in documentation.

styleEvolutions.observation: INV-style-096 (en-dash normalization) is the most frequently enforced invariant across the entire run — triggered rejections, gap remediation, and verification cycles
styleEvolutions.description: Every single task in the run applied INV-style-096. It was the sole or contributing cause of rejection in T-005 (3 en-dashes), drove gap remediation in T-003 (7 en-dashes) and T-006/T-018 (anchor en-dashes). The invariant is correctly specified but the full-page scanning requirement is systematically under-applied by the content-writer. This is not a style evolution but a persistent enforcement gap. Suggests adding an automated pre-submission encoding check.

gapDetails.resolution: T-003 attempt 3 converted all 7 en-dashes. Full binary scan confirmed 0 remaining.

gapDetails.description: En-dashes in 'Attribute Tag Helpers – Images' heading and corresponding anchors across 4 files
gapDetails.resolution: T-018 converted heading in reference-tag-helpers.md and anchors in retrieve-content-items.md and content-types.md. T-006 verified conversion in retrieve-content-items.md. 2 deferred tasks in _guides collection per INV-structure-030.
gapDetails.deferredWork: 2 anchor references in store-files.md (_guides collection) cannot be updated — deferred per INV-structure-030

signal.description: Both gaps were Unicode encoding issues (en-dashes) rather than content coverage gaps. The gap-hunter detected them via binary grep scanning that the content-writer's initial passes missed. All gaps resolved in a single re-entry cycle with 100% first-attempt acceptance. GAP-002 spanned 4 files including 2 in the off-limits _guides collection — proper INV-structure-030 compliance produced deferred tasks rather than violations. Confirms that automated encoding checks should be part of the pre-submission workflow.

complexityCorrelation.observation: Task complexity (acceptance criteria count + content type) correlates with multi-cycle likelihood

createTasks.note: Only task type with code samples — vulnerable to import/namespace accuracy errors

updateTasksHighCriteria.note: T-003's multi-cycle was structural error (duplicate insertion) not complexity-driven

updateTasksLowCriteria.note: All 3 multi-cycle outcomes were mechanical (en-dash detection, gap verification, enhancement) not content quality failures

complexityCorrelation.analysis: The single 'create' task (T-001) with 23 acceptance criteria and code samples was the most challenging and the only task with both style AND accuracy rejections. Update tasks with ≤6 criteria achieved 80% first-attempt success. The 3 multi-cycle update tasks in the low-criteria group were caused by encoding issues (en-dashes) or voluntary enhancement, not content quality failures. This confirms PAT-009: narrow-scope update tasks are inherently lower-risk.

PAT-001.note: T-006 and T-007 had multi-cycle outcomes but for gap remediation/enhancement, not content failures. Effective for focused developer updates.

PAT-002.note: Used in the most complex task. Multi-cycle outcome driven by accuracy issues (code imports) not pattern failure.

PAT-009.note: 100% first-attempt acceptance across all 8 tasks citing PAT-009. Strongest signal in the run. Confirms PAT-009 effectiveness.

PAT-012.note: T-007 had multi-cycle for enhancement, not rejection. Effectively 100% first-attempt reviewer approval.

enDashAntiPattern.currentRun: 3 tasks affected by en-dash detection failures (CAPAT-001)
enDashAntiPattern.priorRuns: Prior run's task-signals.json also documented en-dash persistence as ANTI-002 and GAP-002
enDashAntiPattern.recommendation: Promote to a mandatory pre-submission check in the content-writer workflow. Add automated grep -P '\x{2013}' scan before writer-output.json submission.

crossRefUpdatePattern.currentRun: PAT-009 achieved 100% first-attempt rate across 8 tasks (CPAT-001)
crossRefUpdatePattern.priorRuns: Prior run confirmed PAT-009 with 'near-perfect first-attempt rates'
crossRefUpdatePattern.recommendation: PAT-009 is now validated across 2 consecutive runs. Promote to stable pattern.
